http:listener-config.name: HTTP_Listener_config
http:listener-config.doc:name: HTTP Listener Config
http:listener-config.doc:description: HTTP listener on port 8081 for MCP /mcp endpoint

mcp:server-config.name: mcpServerConfig
mcp:server-config.doc:name: MCP Server Config
mcp:server-config.doc:description: MCP Server using Streamable HTTP transport for AI agent tool calls and human-in-the-loop elicitation

http:request-config.name: Refund_API_Config
http:request-config.doc:name: Refund API Config
http:request-config.doc:description: Downstream refund API - called only after human APPROVE confirmation

flow.name: issue-refund-flow
flow.doc:name: Issue Refund Flow
flow.doc:description: 4-stage refund flow: receive tool call, validate, elicit human approval for high-value refunds, execute only on APPROVE

mcp:tool-listener.name: issue_refund
mcp:tool-listener.doc:name: issue_refund Tool Listener
mcp:tool-listener.doc:description: MCP tool listener exposing issue_refund to AI agents
mcp:tool-listener.mcp:description: Issue a refund for a customer order. Validates eligibility, checks duplicates, assesses risk, and requires human approval for amounts over 50,000 units. Inputs: orderId (string), amount (number), currency (ISO-4217), reason (string).
mcp:tool-listener.mcp:parameters-schema: {"type":"object","properties":{"orderId":{"type":"string","description":"Order ID (e.g. ORD-10482)"},"amount":{"type":"number","description":"Amount in smallest unit (paise for INR)","minimum":1},"currency":{"type":"string","description":"ISO 4217 code e.g. INR","pattern":"^[A-Z]{3}$"},"reason":{"type":"string","description":"Refund reason","minLength":5,"maxLength":500}},"required":["orderId","amount","currency","reason"]}

set-variable.doc:name: Store Session ID
set-variable.doc:description: Saves MCP session ID before payload transform - required for create-elicitation

set-variable.doc:name: Store Refund Request
set-variable.doc:description: Preserves raw tool call payload for downstream reference

ee:transform.doc:name: Validate and Compute Approval Flag
ee:transform.doc:description: Eligibility, duplicate, threshold and fraud checks. requiresApproval = amount>50000 OR riskFlag

ee:message.ee:set-payload: %dw 2.0 output application/java var amount = payload.amount as Number var isEligible = true var existingRefund = false var riskFlag = amount > 100000 var policyResult = if (!isEligible) "FAIL_INELIGIBLE" else if (existingRefund) "FAIL_DUPLICATE" else "PASS" --- { orderId: payload.orderId, amount: amount, currency: payload.currency, reason: payload.reason, validation: { eligibilityCheck: if (isEligible) "PASS" else "FAIL", duplicateCheck: if (!existingRefund) "NONE" else "DUPLICATE_FOUND", amountThreshold: if (amount > 50000) "EXCEEDED" else "WITHIN_LIMIT", riskLevel: if (riskFlag) "HIGH-VALUE REFUND" else "STANDARD", riskFlag: riskFlag, policyCheckResult: policyResult }, requiresApproval: (amount > 50000) or riskFlag }

logger.message: #['[Refund] order=' ++ payload.orderId ++ ' policy=' ++ payload.validation.policyCheckResult ++ ' requiresApproval=' ++ (payload.requiresApproval as String)]
logger.doc:name: Log Validation

choice.doc:name: Policy Check Gate
choice.doc:description: Deny immediately on policy failure; otherwise route to approval logic

set-variable.doc:name: Set Policy Denial
set-variable.doc:description: Records policy denial message as the refund result

logger.doc:name: Log Denial
logger.doc:description: Logs policy denial for compliance monitoring

set-variable.doc:name: Store Validated Refund
set-variable.doc:description: Stores validated refund data for elicitation message and downstream processing

choice.doc:name: Requires Human Approval?
choice.doc:description: High-value or high-risk refunds (amount>50000 or riskFlag) route through human approval elicitation

mcp:create-elicitation.message: #['Refund Approval Required | Order: ' ++ vars.validatedRefund.orderId ++ ' | Amount: ' ++ vars.validatedRefund.currency ++ ' ' ++ (vars.validatedRefund.amount as String) ++ ' | Risk: ' ++ vars.validatedRefund.validation.riskLevel ++ ' | Policy: ' ++ vars.validatedRefund.validation.policyCheckResult ++ ' | Reason: ' ++ vars.validatedRefund.reason]
mcp:create-elicitation.doc:name: Create Elicitation - Refund Approval
mcp:create-elicitation.doc:description: Sends approval form to MCP client. Reviewer sees order/amount/risk details and must APPROVE or REJECT with reason. ACCEPT+APPROVE executes refund; all else fails closed.

logger.doc:name: Log Elicitation Response
logger.doc:description: Logs elicitation action and decision for audit trail

choice.doc:name: Process Elicitation Response
choice.doc:description: CRITICAL: ACCEPT+APPROVE=execute. ACCEPT+REJECT=record rejection. DECLINE/other=fail closed. CANCEL handled by error handler.

http:request.doc:name: Execute Refund API
http:request.doc:description: Calls downstream refund API with approved refund details and correlation ID
http:request.http:body: #[output application/json --- { orderId: vars.validatedRefund.orderId, amount: vars.validatedRefund.amount, currency: vars.validatedRefund.currency, reason: vars.validatedRefund.reason, approvalDecision: vars.elicitationResult.content.decision, reviewerReason: vars.elicitationResult.content.reviewerReason, correlationId: correlationId }]

ee:transform.doc:name: Format Success Response
ee:transform.doc:description: Formats successful refund response with approval details

ee:message.ee:set-payload: %dw 2.0 output application/json --- { status: "SUCCESS", message: "Refund of " ++ vars.validatedRefund.currency ++ " " ++ (vars.validatedRefund.amount as String) ++ " was approved and successfully issued.", orderId: vars.validatedRefund.orderId, refundId: (payload.refundId default correlationId), reviewerReason: vars.elicitationResult.content.reviewerReason, correlationId: correlationId }

set-variable.doc:name: Set Success Result
set-variable.doc:description: Stores JSON success response as refundResult for the MCP tool response

logger.message: #['[Refund] APPROVED and EXECUTED - order=' ++ vars.validatedRefund.orderId ++ ' by reviewer: ' ++ (vars.elicitationResult.content.reviewerReason default '')]
logger.doc:name: Log Approval Execution
logger.doc:description: Logs successful refund execution with reviewer information

set-variable.value: #['Refund rejected by reviewer. Order: ' ++ vars.validatedRefund.orderId ++ '. Reviewer reason: ' ++ (vars.elicitationResult.content.reviewerReason default 'No reason provided')]
set-variable.doc:name: Set Rejection Result
set-variable.doc:description: Records reviewer rejection - refund is NOT executed

logger.doc:name: Log Reviewer Rejection
logger.doc:description: Logs reviewer rejection for audit and compliance

set-variable.value: #['Refund not executed. Reviewer action: ' ++ vars.elicitationResult.action ++ '. Order: ' ++ vars.validatedRefund.orderId ++ '. No refund was processed.']
set-variable.doc:name: Set Decline Result
set-variable.doc:description: Fails closed on DECLINE or unexpected action - refund is NOT executed

logger.doc:name: Log Decline
logger.doc:description: Logs DECLINE or unexpected elicitation action

http:request.doc:name: Execute Direct Refund
http:request.doc:description: Executes low-value standard refund directly without human approval
http:request.http:body: #[output application/json --- { orderId: vars.validatedRefund.orderId, amount: vars.validatedRefund.amount, currency: vars.validatedRefund.currency, reason: vars.validatedRefund.reason, approvalDecision: "AUTO_APPROVED", correlationId: correlationId }]

ee:message.ee:set-payload: %dw 2.0 output application/json --- { status: "SUCCESS", message: "Refund of " ++ vars.validatedRefund.currency ++ " " ++ (vars.validatedRefund.amount as String) ++ " was auto-approved and successfully issued.", orderId: vars.validatedRefund.orderId, refundId: (payload.refundId default correlationId), correlationId: correlationId }

set-variable.doc:name: Set Direct Refund Result
set-variable.doc:description: Stores auto-approved refund response as refundResult for MCP tool response

error-handler.doc:name: Error Handler
error-handler.doc:description: Handles elicitation timeout (fail closed), HTTP API errors, and unexpected errors

on-error-continue.doc:name: Handle Elicitation Timeout
on-error-continue.doc:description: Elicitation timed out waiting for reviewer response - escalate or fail closed, never execute refund

set-variable.value: #['Refund approval timed out. Order: ' ++ (vars.validatedRefund.orderId default vars.refundRequest.orderId default 'unknown') ++ '. No refund was processed. Please retry or escalate.']
set-variable.doc:name: Set Timeout Result
set-variable.doc:description: Records timeout outcome - refund is NOT executed

logger.message: #['[Refund] TIMEOUT - elicitation timed out, order=' ++ (vars.validatedRefund.orderId default 'unknown')]
logger.doc:name: Log Timeout
logger.doc:description: Logs elicitation timeout for escalation tracking

set-variable.value: #['Refund API unavailable. Order: ' ++ (vars.validatedRefund.orderId default 'unknown') ++ '. Please retry. Error: ' ++ error.description]
set-variable.doc:name: Set API Error Result

on-error-continue.doc:name: Handle Unexpected Errors
on-error-continue.doc:description: Catch-all for unexpected errors including MCP:OPERATION_CANCELLED from CANCEL action

set-variable.value: #['Refund operation failed unexpectedly. Order: ' ++ (vars.validatedRefund.orderId default vars.refundRequest.orderId default 'unknown') ++ '. No refund was processed. Error: ' ++ error.description]
set-variable.doc:name: Set Unexpected Error Result
set-variable.doc:description: Records unexpected error outcome - refund is NOT executed
